title: Fetoscopic surgery training simulator for the treatment of spina bifida
abstract: The simulator (100) comprises at least one uterus model (200) and at least one fetal model (300), which includes a uterus body (210) with an opening (215); a receptacle (220) for at least partially accommodating the uterus body (210); and at least one laparoscopic port (230) intended to receive at least one instrument and adapted to be placed in the opening (215) of the uterus body(210). The fetal model (300) includes a fetal body (310) having at least one cavity (320), at least one defect model (500) intended to be at least partially accommodated in said cavity (320) of the fetal body (310), and a placenta model (400) intended to be placed in the uterus body (210) to maintain the fetal body (310) in a stable position.
claims:
Claims 1- A simulator (100) for training of fetoscopic surgery interventions for the treatment of spina bifida, comprising at least one uterus model (200) and at least one fetus model (300), characterized in that the uterus model (200) includes: - a uterus body (210) with an opening (215); - a receptacle (220) for at least partially accommodating the uterus body (210); and - at least one laparoscopic port (230) intended to receive at least one instrument and adapted to be placed in the opening (215) of the uterus body (210); and in that the fetal model (300) includes: - a fetal body (310) having at least one cavity (320); - at least one defect model (500) intended to be at least partially accommodated in said cavity (320) of the fetal body (310); and - a placenta model (400) intended to be placed in the uterus body (210) to hold the fetus body (310) in a stable position. 2- The simulator (100) of claim 1 , wherein the defect model (500) comprises: - a muscular layer model intended to be fixed to the fetal body (310); - a body simulating a defect with a liquid-filled balloon attached to the muscle layer and with a spinal cord model attached to the liquid-filled balloon; and - a skin layer model attached to the fetal body (310). 3- The simulator (100) of claim 2, wherein the body simulating a defect is a representation of a defect selected from the group consisting of: myelomeningocele, myelocele, meningocele, lipomyelomeningocele. 4- The simulator (100) of any of the claims 1-3, wherein it comprises means for infusing CO2 into the uterus body (210). 5- The simulator (100) of any of the claims 1-4, wherein it further comprises at least one laparoscopic tower with at least one display screen, at least one camera, at least 

 one light source, laparoscopic surgical instruments, and at least one fetoscopic instrument. 6- The simulator (100) of any of claims 1-5, wherein the fetal model (300) is a three- dimensional reproduction of a fetus at 20-27 weeks of gestation. 7- The simulator (100) of any of the claims 1-6, wherein the uterus body (210) is a 15 to 35 cm diameter spherical body. 8 - The simulator (100) of any of the claims 1-7, wherein the uterus body (210) is made of a soft and compressible material. 9- The simulator (100) of any of the claims 8, wherein the uterus body (210) is compressible at pressures of the order of 6-15 mmHg. 10- The simulator (100) of any of the claims 1-9, wherein the uterus body (210) is made of a material resistant to water or to any colloidal or crystalloid liquid. 11- The simulator (100) of any of the claims 1-10, wherein the opening of the uterus body (210) is 6 to 10 cm in diameter. 12 - The simulator (100) of any of the claims 1-11 , wherein it comprises means for attaching the uterus body (210) to the receptacle (220). 13 - The simulator (100) of any of claims 1-12, wherein the laparoscopic port (230) is configured to allow the introduction of liquids and/or gas before and/or during surgery. 14- The simulator (100) of any of the claims 1-13, wherein it comprises a support base (240), with the receptacle (220) being attached to said support base (240). 15- A simulation method of a surgical training practice of minimally invasive neonatal fetoscopic surgery operations for the treatment of spina bifida using a simulator (100) as claimed any of the claims 1-14, characterized in that it comprises introducing the placenta model (400) inside the uterus body (210) and the fetal body (310) with the defect model (500), and subsequently closing the opening (215) of the uterus body (210) with the laparoscopic port (230). 

Simulator for training of fetoscopic surgery operations for the treatment of spina bifida
